sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: For centuries, the depths of the ocean were regarded as presenting (1) ...... (MOUNT) challenges to any form of exploration...
...given that pressure, darkness and low temperatures made conditions far from (2) ...... (FAVOUR) for human divers.
The invention of the submersible prompted a complete (3) ...... (ASSESS) of what was scientifically achievable underwater.
Researchers have discovered creatures thriving in (4) ...... (SEEM) inhospitable environments on the ocean floor.
(5) ...... (PROBABLE) as it sounds, entire ecosystems flourish around hydrothermal vents that release scalding water...
...in what can only be described as an underwater (6) ...... (WILD) untouched by sunlight.
Many shallow-water species are now (7) ...... (THREAT) as rising ocean temperatures disrupt their habitats...
...with coral reefs particularly close to (8) ...... (EXTINCT) in certain regions of the world.
Scientists warn that this type of (9) ...... (ECOLOGY) collapse could have devastating consequences for global food chains.
The deep sea remains one of the most (10) ...... (MYSTERY) frontiers left on our planet.

sections.title: B: Multiple Choice (Conditionals)
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: We wouldn't have missed the train if you **haven't / hadn't** taken so long to get ready.
If you **would / should** happen to see Clara at the conference, could you give her this message?
Let's download the maps beforehand **in case / unless** we lose our internet connection.
If you had double-checked the figures, you wouldn't **have / have had** to redo the entire report.
If she **was / had been** born a century earlier, would Marie Curie have made the same discoveries?
**Unless / Otherwise** we leave by seven, we're going to hit the morning rush hour.
The performance would have been a disaster **but for / if not** the lead actor's remarkable improvisation.
We can reschedule the meeting for Friday, **supposing / providing** everyone is available that day.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
The only reason she didn't accept the promotion was the need to relocate. **for**
Had ...... , she would have accepted the promotion.
items.answer: it not been for the relocation

items.sentence:
Why do you think the project didn't succeed? **down**
What do you ...... to?

Clara felt unwell this morning so she stayed at home instead of going to work. **weather**
Clara was ...... this morning so she stayed at home.

If the manager reduced unnecessary spending, the team would trust him more. **to**
Were ...... spending, the team would trust him more.

The vet's advice was to avoid interfering and the injured bird would recover on its own. **nature**
The vet said that we should ...... and the bird would recover.

If the research team can collaborate effectively, they should meet the deadline. **so**
The research team should meet the deadline, ...... collaborate effectively.

I made a small error early on, so my entire presentation went wrong. **slipped**
My entire presentation would have gone well ...... early on.

There's a strong possibility that Laura will be selected for the scholarship. **being**
Laura's ...... for the scholarship are strong.

sections.title: D: Idioms / Collocations (Gap Fill)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Throughout the conference, the organisers put all the necessary resources ...... the delegates' disposal.
She managed to finish the marathon ...... dint of months of gruelling daily training.
...... the risk of sounding impatient, could you tell us when the results will be announced?
There's no clock in this room, but ...... a guess I'd say it's around three in the afternoon.
Marcus is a passionate sailor who goes out on the water ...... all weathers.
I had completely forgotten my wallet, but ...... luck would have it there was a cash machine just round the corner.
She rehearsed answers to every possible question so as not to leave anything ...... chance on the day.
My grandmother rarely gambles, but she does occasionally place a small amount ...... a horse race.
The unexpected delay has ...... up several complications for the project team.
We ...... in the mountains now if we hadn't cancelled the trip at the last minute.
She apologised for the comment, insisting that it hadn't been ...... .
The local team won the championship ...... all expectations.
Since they're planning a trip abroad, they're ...... back on dining out this month.
It would have been a perfect evening, ...... the power not gone out during the concert.
No one wanted to break the bad news to Sarah, but I drew the short ...... and had to tell her.
Authorities warned that the dam could collapse, so residents were ...... from the surrounding villages.
